codeReview.summary: Comprehensive port of notebook/scribe processing methods from yj_to_epub_notebook.py:78-614 to Go. The implementation achieves strong parity with the Python source across all 8 VAL-M4-NB assertions. All 44 targeted tests pass. The code is well-structured, correctly handles brush type classification, SVG path generation, PNG density map generation, stroke value decoding, and annotation content processing. A few minor non-blocking parity observations noted below.

issues.description: PNG density map: Python uses integer division for midpoint calculation (x1 + x2) // 2, but Go uses integer division (x1 + x2) / 2 which is equivalent. However, the Python uses integer types for r1, r2, d1, d2 in add_points_if_needed while Go uses float64. The Python's (r1 + r2) // 2 and (d1 + d2) // 2 perform integer division, but Go does (r1 + r2) / 2.0 and (d1 + d2) / 2.0 as float division. This is actually correct since Go's radius and density are already float64 (from t/scaleFactor and daf/100), matching the Python behavior where r0 = t / (PNG_SCALE_FACTOR * 2) which is float division. Non-blocking because the end behavior is equivalent.

PNG generation: Python uses PIL Image.new('P' if stroke_color != 0 else '1') with a palette and transparency=1, while Go always uses image.NewRGBA. This means Go generates RGBA PNGs instead of palette-mode or binary-mode PNGs. The visual result is equivalent but the PNG encoding differs (Go produces larger files). This is an acceptable adaptation since Go's image/png doesn't support mode '1' or palette mode with transparency in the same way.

processNotebookContent: Python raises an Exception for unknown data types (line 233: raise Exception(...)), while Go logs an error and returns. This is an acceptable adaptation since Go doesn't use exceptions, but it means the Go version is more lenient — it won't halt processing on bad data. The handoff document notes this as an intentional deviation.

processNotebookContent: The Python code calls self.check_empty() at the end of process_notebook_content (line 268) to verify all content keys were consumed. The Go implementation does not have an equivalent check_empty call. This means unconsumed keys won't be logged as warnings. This is a minor parity gap that could be addressed later when integrating with the full book processing pipeline.

scribeNotebookAnnotation: Python calls self.check_empty(story, ...) and self.check_empty(annotation, ...) at lines 551-552, but Go does not call any equivalent. This means unconsumed annotation keys won't be detected. Same pattern as processNotebookContent — minor parity gap.

scribeAnnotationContent: Python uses content.pop('$145', '') which defaults to empty string, Go uses contentMap["$145"] with a type assertion that defaults to empty string — functionally equivalent. However, Python also calls self.check_empty(style_event, ...) and self.check_empty(content, ...) which Go omits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The check_empty pattern from Python (verifying all dict/map keys were consumed) is consistently omitted across all notebook processing functions. If this is intentional (deferred to integration testing), it should be documented in the library as a known parity gap. If not, a helper function like checkEmpty(map[string]interface{}, string) could be added.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Python yj_to_epub_notebook.py lines 268, 551-552, 578, 613 all call self.check_empty(). Go yj_to_epub_notebook.go has no equivalent calls.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The d1-notebook-scribe.md library note is comprehensive and was updated in the same work session. It correctly documents the architecture, brush type dual behavior, PNG density map details, and validation contract discrepancies. This is well-maintained shared state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/d1-notebook-scribe.md updated in commit 2d65777 with 35 new lines documenting the implementation architecture.

summary: PASS. The m4-notebook-scribe feature successfully ports all 8 notebook/scribe process methods from yj_to_epub_notebook.py:78-614 to Go with strong 1:1 parity. All 44 targeted tests pass with zero regressions. The implementation correctly handles: processNotebookContent dispatching ($270 layouts, $146/$176 recursion), scribeNotebookStroke with stroke groups and SVG <g> elements, SVG path generation with M/L commands and stroke-width attributes, PNG density map generation with base64 encoding, brush type classification with correct names and opacity values, scribeNotebookAnnotation handling HWR annotations with <text>/<tspan> elements, decodeStrokeValues edge cases, and adjustColorForDensity grayscale values. Non-blocking observations: (1) Python's check_empty calls are omitted, (2) PNG generation uses RGBA instead of PIL's palette/binary modes, (3) Python exceptions are adapted to Go error logs. These are acceptable deviations documented in the library notes.
